body.title: Investigate wg_meta upsert latency (5,855ms worst, 143 hits)
body.result: 37665892 — batched word_count upserts (G-WG3 doc_count pattern). _inc_word_count + flush_word_count. Root cause: every index() hit the hot wg_meta row; 500% bloat on 2-row table confirmed pre-fix. 7 tests.
body.description:
INSERT INTO wg_meta (key, value) ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE hits 5,855ms worst-case across 143 calls. This is an investigation ticket — find the root cause (lock contention, table bloat, missing index on wg_meta, or write amplification) and either fix in-place if trivial or spawn a follow-on ticket.

**Affected files:** TBD discovery — likely wild_igor/igor/memory/word_graph.py or lab/utility_closet/wg_*.py. Update docstring on the upsert call site once identified.
**Design rules:** no-sqlite: touches wg_meta table (Postgres existing). oop-first: investigation only, no new shared state in this ticket. docs-in-code: update docstring on upsert call site when found. test-plan-or-why-not: applies — see below.
**Scope boundary:** IN — identify root cause, fix if trivial OR file follow-on ticket with concrete fix shape. OUT — rewriting word graph, changing wg_meta schema without evidence.
**Test plan:** No new tests in investigation phase. Deliverable: root-cause writeup in ticket close note. If fix is trivial (index gap, single-line guard), add before/after timing assertion in test_word_graph*.py. If non-trivial, spawn follow-on with its own test plan.
